title: How to Cite a TikTok or YouTube Video in MLA Format
author: Tklesson Team
content:
You found a TikTok that perfectly supports your argument. You watched a YouTube documentary for your history paper. Now you need to cite them. Your teacher gave you an MLA guide, but it was printed in 2009. It doesn't mention social media. Here is how to cite TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, and other modern sources in MLA format.

The Basic Formula for Online Video

Author. 'Title of Video.' Website, uploaded by Username, Date, URL.

Works for YouTube, TikTok, Vimeo, and most video platforms. The same formula works for all of them.

Citing a YouTube Video

Last Name, First Name of creator (if known). 'Title of Video.' YouTube, uploaded by Channel Name, Date, URL.

Example: Green, John. 'The Economics of Healthcare.' YouTube, uploaded by CrashCourse, 15 Sept. 2021, www.youtube.com/watch?v=abc123.

If the creator's real name is not available, use the channel name as the author.

Example: 'The Economics of Healthcare.' YouTube, uploaded by CrashCourse, 15 Sept. 2021, www.youtube.com/watch?v=abc123.

Citing a TikTok Video

Author's Last Name, First Name (if known). 'Title of Video.' TikTok, uploaded by Username, Date, URL.

Example: Chen, Emily. 'How to Make a Perfect Omelet.' TikTok, uploaded by @emilyskitchen, 10 Jan. 2024, www.tiktok.com/@emilyskitchen/video/123456.

TikTok usernames include the @ symbol. Keep it in your citation. The date should be when the video was posted, not when you viewed it.

If the video has no title, describe it in brackets. Example: [Video of baking cookies].

Citing an Instagram Post

Author. 'Caption or Description.' Instagram, Date, URL.

Example: National Geographic. 'Photo of aurora borealis over Iceland.' Instagram, 5 Feb. 2024, www.instagram.com/p/abc123.

If the post has no caption, describe it in brackets. Example: [Infographic about climate change].

In-Text Citations

For in-text citations, use the author's last name or the first word of the title. Same as any other source.

Example: (Green) or ('The Economics of Healthcare').

No page number needed. You're citing a timestamped source, but MLA doesn't require timestamps for most video citations. If you're quoting a specific moment, include the timestamp in the in-text citation: (Green 02:15-02:30).

What About AI-Generated Content?

MLA now has a format for citing AI. 'Title of conversation.' Name of AI tool, Version, Company, Date, URL.

Example: 'Summary of photosynthesis.' ChatGPT, 4 Apr. 2025, chat.openai.com/share/abc123.

AI is not a traditional source. Check with your professor before using AI-generated content in your paper. Many do not allow it.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

Don't use the viewer's name as the author. The creator is the author. You are the viewer.

Don't forget the access date. MLA now recommends including the date you viewed the source, especially for content that might change. Add it at the end: Accessed 15 Mar. 2025.

Don't use the URL shortener. Use the full URL. It's ugly. MLA requires it.

Citing social media is new. Your teacher will appreciate that you're making the effort. Cite carefully. Keep your bibliography clean.
